free_text: Experimental study of the T = Tz states in 90Zr via a
(3He,d) reaction
Florida A&M University, Tallahassee
Experiment performed using 3He beam
from the Oak Ridge isochronous cyclotron.
Self-supported 89Y foil with thickness of 292 mu-g/cm2.
Outgoing deuterons were momentum analyzed
using Oak Ridge broad-range spectrograph, an uniform
single wedged magnet. The spectrograph magnetic field
was 8.5 kG, the acceptance angle in the reaction plane
was 3deg.
Reaction products were recorded at each angle
by four nuclear emulsion plates of the type Kodak NTB
50 mu, placed along the focal plane of the
spectrograph. Spectra were measured at 12 angles: 5,
7.5 deg, and in 5deg intervals from 10deg to 55deg.
Deuteron energy range obtained by 4 plates was 16 MeV.
Compiled by S.H.
Differential cross sections taken from fig. 4
of the reference. Angles of measurements taken from
text.
No information given
